title: LexisNexis and ALPR Contracting Documents - Hamet PD (CA)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

On May 20th, the DEA and IRS-CI filed a statement in United States v. Pham [4:26-mj-04166, (D. Mass. May 20, 2026) ECF No. 95 - https://www.courtlistener.com/docket/73348335/95/1/united-states-v-pham/]. This document indicated that:
A) Federal agents have access to local Automated License Plate Recognition (ALPR) systems operating in the city of Hamet
B) The ALPR system that federal agents have access to appear to be mobile (mounted on vehicles)
C) Federal agents have access to police records that show prior contacts at a local Hamet address

To better understand this filing by federal agents, we are requesting the following documents:
1) Is your agency contributing to the Public Safety Data Exchange? 
 - Documents: Any signed contracts, NDAs, Schedule A, or addendums between Hamet Police and LexisNexis (including LexisNexis Risk Solutions, Accurint, AVCC, etc.). This includes documentation on what data you provide to LexisNexis for them to host your CommunityCrimeMap.
2) Is Hamet PD part of a law enforcement consortium?
 - Documents: Any MOUs or other document that your agency signed to join a regional law enforcement consortium (if any)
3) What local ALPRs in Hamet were federal agents querying for their investigation, and were the systems run by Hamet PD or private? 
 - Documents: Any ALPR policy, ALPR training manual, or contract agreement with a vendor (Flock, Motorola/Vigilant, etc.) that would provide insight into what systems are present, AND how they are shared (i.e. does local data flow into PSDEX/Accurint?) 

Written statements are sufficient to fulfil this request - however some agencies choose to only reply with documents, so I provided the relevant documents requested as well. I am not seeking active case information or PII. I am only seeking to understand in what way local law enforcement data and citizen location info is available to federal agencies.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal
